alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices & Services leading commercial operations and transformation programs; two promotions demonstrating strategy-setting capability; positioning as Strategy and Operations leader
alignments.job_requirement: Define and execute OpenAI's EMEA GTM partnerships strategy, operating model, and business objectives

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google experience scaling global commercial operations; no explicit partner-ecosystem or co-delivery program evidence in profile; this is a core strength gap
alignments.job_requirement: Own partner-sourced, partner-influenced, and partner-delivered business outcomes across EMEA; Partner closely with regional sales leadership to create scalable co-sell and co-delivery motions

alignments.candidate_evidence: Scaling operations and transformation programs at Google suggests operational rigor; no direct evidence of partner enablement, certification programs, or third-party ecosystem governance
alignments.job_requirement: Establish governance frameworks, operating rhythms, and performance management systems for the partner ecosystem; drive partner enablement and accountability mechanisms

alignments.candidate_evidence: Two promotions at Google and leadership positioning; no explicit team leadership scope, headcount managed, or organization-building evidence provided
alignments.job_requirement: Recruit, develop, and lead a high-performing team of GTM Partner Directors across EMEA; build culture centered on customer outcomes and accountability

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google Devices & Services global scope; no explicit evidence of alliance program management or executive stakeholder engagement at partner-organization level (CEOs, Managing Partners)
alignments.job_requirement: Have built and managed large-scale strategic alliance programs across multiple countries and markets; operate with senior executives including CEOs, Managing Partners, CIOs, CTOs

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program experience at Google; no consulting, services delivery, or professional services economics background indicated
alignments.job_requirement: Understand enterprise delivery models, consulting economics, and how services organizations drive technology adoption; familiarity with enterprise AI adoption and transformation programs

gaps.gap: Limited direct experience building and managing large-scale strategic alliance or partnership programs with GSIs, consulting firms, and services organizations
gaps.mitigation: Hire embedded partner operations specialist; partner with GTM VP and Sales leadership for alliance framework best practices; establish partnerships mentorship or advisory relationship with peer alliance leader at hyperscaler or enterprise software vendor

gaps.gap: No demonstrated background in consulting economics, professional services delivery models, or how services organizations operationalize and monetize technology partnerships
gaps.mitigation: Invest in structured onboarding on consulting economics and services delivery; work closely with Finance, Services, and existing partner organizations to learn margin, utilization, and delivery-risk models; hire or partner with a services operations expert

gaps.gap: No explicit evidence of leading cross-functional executive engagement with external C-suite stakeholders (CEOs, Managing Partners, CIOs) or managing regional reporting complexity
gaps.mitigation: Leverage OpenAI executive relationships and Sales leadership to co-drive initial partner calls; develop executive engagement playbook with Communications/Marketing; pair with GTM VP for executive relationship coaching

gaps.gap: Team leadership scope not explicitly detailed; no clear evidence of managing direct reports, org design, or hiring at scale
gaps.mitigation: Clarify expected team size and structure in first 30 days; establish weekly 1:1s and feedback loops with new team; partner with People Operations for hiring and development infrastructure

gaps.gap: No prior experience with partner enablement, certification programs, performance management frameworks, or KPI accountability systems specific to ecosystem partners
gaps.mitigation: Study existing partner certifications in industry (e.g., cloud provider models); hire Director of Partner Operations to lead enablement/governance; leverage cross-functional operations expertise from Google tenure

output.level_rationale: JD explicitly requires 15+ years enterprise technology/partnerships/ecosystem leadership; scope includes building and leading a distributed team across Europe, owning regional GTM strategy and partner-driven revenue, and operating at CEO/C-suite level with external executives. This is manager-of-managers scope with significant business ownership, indicating L6. Confidence reduced slightly because company stage/size context is not stated in JD, though OpenAI's scale supports the estimate.
output.summary: Strong strategic and operations background with eight years of Google experience and two promotions positions the candidate well for GTM strategy and transformation scope. However, this role is an L6-stretch requiring 15+ years of alliance/partnership program management, services economics, and third-party ecosystem stewardship—areas where candidate evidence is thin. No evidence of consulting, GSI relationships, or partner certification/enablement programs; regional distribution and partner-driven revenue ownership are unclear. Gaps are learnable but nontrivial; manageability depends on partner support and ramp timeline.
uncertainties: Profile does not specify headcount managed, team leadership breadth, or organizational design experience at Google
No information on candidate's familiarity with Salesforce, partner management systems, or alliance tracking infrastructure
Extent of executive-level external stakeholder management (with partner CEOs, Managing Partners) is not stated; Google experience may be internal-facing
Company stage/size context for OpenAI is not stated in JD, which slightly limits L6 confidence even though role scope and seniority requirements are explicit
No evidence of success in regional expansion, market entry, or navigating EMEA regulatory/cultural complexity
